LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KIRSTIN OAKES' MOTION TO VACATE AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT KIRSTIN OAKES' "MOTION TO VACATE AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S RENEWAL OF JUDGEMENT" IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION IS NOT IN PROPER FORMAT AND THERE IS NO PROOF THAT DEFENDANT SERVED IT ON THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR, DEFENDANT EVIDENTLY IS SEEKING TO VACATE THE RENEWED JUDGMENT AGAINST HER IN THE AMOUNT OF $17,466.81 THAT WAS ENTERED ON DECEMBER 11, 2019 ON THE GROUND THAT SHE WAS NOT AWARE OF THE NOTICE OF RENEWAL HEARING ON THAT DATE. HOWEVER, NO SUCH HEARING WAS HELD. RATHER, THE COURT CLERK ENTERED THE RENEWAL OF THE JUDGMENT IN THE COURT RECORDS. (CODE CIV. PROC. 683.150.) THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR WAS REQUIRED TO SERVE NOTICE OF RENEWAL OF THE JUDGMENT ON THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR, WHICH IT DID ON DECEMBER 11, 2019. (CODE CIV. PROC. 683.160.) THAT THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR DID NOT NOTIFY DEFENDANT OF A HEARING THAT WAS NOT HELD (OR REQUIRED TO BE HELD) IS NOT GROUNDS FOR VACATING OR MODIFYING THE RENEWED JUDGMENT.JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Vacate Against Plaintiff'S Renewal Of Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KIRSTIN OAKES' Motion To Vacate Against Plaintiff'S Renewal Of Judgment.

Defendant Kirstin Oakes' "motion to vacate against Plaintiff's Renewal of Judgement" is denied. Although the motion is not in proper format and there is no proof that defendant served it on the judgment creditor, defendant evidently is seeking to vacate the renewed judgment against her in the amount of $17,466.81 that was entered on December 11, 2019 on the ground that she was not aware of the notice of renewal hearing on that date. However, no such hearing was held. Rather, the court clerk entered the renewal of the judgment in the court records. (Code Civ. Proc. 683.150.) The judgment creditor was required to serve notice of renewal of the judgment on the judgment debtor, which it did on December 11, 2019. (Code Civ. Proc. 683.160.) That the judgment creditor did not notify defendant of a hearing that was not held (or required to be held) is not grounds for vacating or modifying the renewed judgment.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
